blocks.content: [3. Lab - The Hardened Install (AGV Easy Install)]
**Learning Objective:** Successfully install Google Antigravity on a secure Linux VM using a 1-click interactive installation script, bypassing the need for Docker or restricted repositories.
**1. Verify Cloudtop VM** Ensure your Cloudtop VM is provisioned and active. Open your local terminal.

**2. Run the AGV Easy Install Script** We will use the unofficial AGV Easy Install manager by wtg-codes. This script auto-detects your environment and recommends the best method. Execute the following command in your terminal:
💡 **Instructor Comment:** If curl fails, ensure you have an active internet connection. If the gum UI fails to download, the script will gracefully fall back to a plain text menu.
**3. Navigate the Interactive Menu** Once the script launches, you will see a hierarchical menu system.

items: When prompted for the installation method, select **Official Binary (.tar.gz)**.

blocks.content: 🛑 **Security Note:** While the script supports APT/DNF and Homebrew, selecting the Official Binary method is required for our corporate Cloudtop environment to bypass system repository restrictions.

**4. Locate Application** Once the script successfully completes, it will map the binaries to ~/.local/bin/antigravity. Go back to the desktop, click on the app grid icon on the bottom left, and locate the new Antigravity icon.
